explanation: The property states that globally (G), if the system is in Start-up Mode or Self-Test Mode (bool_exp1), then the patient must not be connected to the breathing circuit (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement that the patient shall not be connected during these initial system states.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in 'Start-up Mode' or 'SelfTest Mode' (bool_exp2), then the patient must not be connected to the breathing circuit (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement that patient connection is forbidden during these initial system phases.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in Start-up Mode or Self-Test Mode (bool_exp2), then the patient shall not be connected to the breathing circuit (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement that the patient must not be connected during the initial setup and self-check phases of the system.

The natural language statement requires that the patient is not connected to the breathing circuit whenever the system is powered on and undergoing start-up and self-test. This translates to a global implication: whenever the system is in 'StartUpMode' or 'SelfTestMode' (representing the 'powered on and through start-up and self-test' condition), the 'patientConnected' state must be false. Template 10, 'G (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3)', perfectly captures this. 'bool_exp2' is defined as 'StartUpMode | SelfTestMode' to cover both initial system states, and 'bool_exp3' is defined as '! patientConnected' to represent the patient not being connected.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is not off (powered on) and is in either Start-up Mode or SelfTest Mode, then the patient must not be connected to the breathing circuit. This directly captures the requirement that the patient shall not be connected when the system is powered on and going through its initialisation and self-test phases.

The property states that globally, it must always be true that if the system is in either Start-up Mode or Self-Test Mode, then the patient is not connected to the breathing circuit. This directly translates the requirement that the patient shall not be connected when the system is powered on and going through start-up and self-test, as 'powered on' is implicitly covered by 'StartUpMode' and 'SelfTestMode'.

The property states that globally, if the system is in either the Start-up Mode or the Self-Test Mode, then the patient must not be connected to the breathing circuit. This directly captures the requirement that the patient should not be connected during these initial system states. The 'powered on' part of the original sentence is implicitly covered as Start-up and Self-Test modes only occur when the system is powered on.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in Start-up Mode or Self-Test Mode (bool_exp2), then the patient must not be connected to the breathing circuit (bool_exp3). This directly translates the requirement that the patient should not be connected during these specific modes.

The property states that globally, it must always be the case that if the system is in Start-up Mode or Self-Test Mode, then the patient is not connected to the breathing circuit. The phrase "when the system is powered on" is implicitly covered by "through start-up and self-test," as these modes are only active when the system is powered on. The "and" connecting "start-up" and "self-test" is interpreted as "or," indicating the patient should not be connected during either of these phases.

The LTL property states that globally, it must always be the case that if the system is in 'Start-up Mode' or 'SelfTest Mode' (representing the 'powered on and through start-up and self-test' condition), then the patient must not be 'connected to the breathing circuit'. This directly translates the requirement that the patient shall not be connected during these initial system states.
